description: Plugin-distributed hooks for ravenclaude-core. Auto-merged with the consumer's session hooks when the plugin is installed. Paths use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} so they resolve to the plugin's install location, not the consumer's project root.

hooks.comment: Auto-format files after agent edits. No-ops silently if no formatter is configured for the file type.

Advisory: warns when an edit to a plugin agent definition file looks like the agent is instructing itself to spawn another sub-agent — only the Team Lead dispatches. Set RC_GUARD_RECURSIVE_SPAWN_STRICT=1 to make this blocking.
Block obviously destructive commands that slipped past the consumer's deny list. Reads the tool call as JSON on stdin; exits 2 to block (exit 1 would not block).

Command review (the Thing) — T2 single-seat tribunal. No-ops (fast grep) unless a category has `thing: on` in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. When a command's category is toggled on, convenes one reviewer (code-reviewer-shaped, via claude -p) for an ALLOW/DENY verdict, writes a Sága-log audit entry, emits hookSpecificOutput. Fails closed (ask) on its own errors; the platform fails OPEN on timeout so the script enforces its own deadline. Proven end-to-end for shell_readonly.
Layout enforcement. Reads .repo-layout.json from the consumer's project root; no-ops silently if the manifest is absent.
If any source files changed this session, remind the Team Lead to run the full test suite before reporting done.

Advisory: warns when a session loads in acceptEdits/bypassPermissions mode, which bypasses the comfort-posture allow/ask/deny rules. The real pin is permissions.defaultMode=default (written by /set-posture). Reads permission_mode from stdin; never blocks.
